qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mirror settings bypass local-path import restrictions and expose server-local repositories
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the mirror settings update and sync path in `setting.go` and `mirror.go`. The advisory targets `internal/route/repo/branch.go:DeleteBranchPost` missing protected/default-branch deletion checks on the web branch-delete route, so the vulnerable control flow and root cause are different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated attachment endpoints allow arbitrary server-side file storage
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns unauthenticated attachment upload and download routes plus attachment persistence in `attachment.go`. The advisory is an authenticated writer-only branch deletion bypass caused by missing protected/default-branch checks in `DeleteBranchPost`, so the code path and root cause do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described protected branch deletion bypass in `internal/route/repo/branch.go:DeleteBranchPost` or an equivalent missing branch-protection check on the branch deletion route.
evidence: The advisory describes `DeleteBranchPost` on the web branch deletion route, where writer-level users can POST directly to delete protected or default branches because the backend omits the protected-branch and default-branch checks.

`mirror_local_path_import_bypass` instead describes a mirror upstream update path that stores attacker-controlled local filesystem remotes and later syncs them; it does not involve branch deletion, protected branch enforcement, or `DeleteBranchPost`.

`unauthenticated_attachment_upload` instead describes public attachment upload and retrieval endpoints that persist attacker-controlled files; it does not involve the `/branches/delete/*` route, writer permissions, or branch protection logic.
